jokes.text: There are these two MSNBC guys we keep seeing around, and we don't know their names, so we call them the Head and the Hair.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about 'the Head'
jokes.punchline: They're nicknamed after their most prominent physical features
jokes.explanation: Clever premise that sets up the episode's central joke structure. Economical and character-appropriate shallow observation.

jokes.text: That's a Japanese pie watch. It tells time with those little pie pieces. Each piece is six minutes. So right now, it's six times four.
jokes.setup: Gray shows off his watch
jokes.punchline: It's an unnecessarily complicated way to tell time with pie pieces
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd character detail that perfectly establishes Gray as a pretentious nerd. The pie metaphor is wonderfully specific and ridiculous.

jokes.text: 5:30. That can't be right. My watch has these little hands that go around and point at numbers.
jokes.setup: Gray's overcomplicated pie watch explanation
jokes.punchline: Liz's matter-of-fact description of a normal watch as if it's novel
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that highlights the absurdity through contrast. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, that's awesome, possum.
jokes.setup: Response to Liz's watch description
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'awesome possum' as casual response
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyme humor, fits the workplace banter but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: A talk show without a host, just the voice of the dead lady from Desperate Housewives, or a reality show with a lot of super hot nannies who move into a house and help fat kids lose weight?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking for opinions on show ideas
jokes.punchline: Two deliberately terrible and exploitative TV concepts
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of terrible TV programming. Perfect for Tracy's character to pitch exploitative reality shows.

jokes.text: This television programming stuff is just one unpredictable ass ache, and I want you to get it out of here.
jokes.setup: Tracy dismissing the programming discussion
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate description of TV development
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's impatience with industry nonsense in his typical blunt style.

jokes.text: I'm going to be your bottom, Kenneth. And I want you to ride me as hard as you can.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the 'Bottoms Up' corporate program
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sexual phrasing in business context
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre that works because Jack is completely oblivious to the sexual implications.

jokes.text: They had girls dancing in cages. And not the go-go cages, the little dog cages that you crate pit bulls in.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing a party
jokes.punchline: Escalates from go-go cages to actual dog cages
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that subverts expectations. The specificity of 'pit bull crates' makes it more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: That is awesome. - That is upsetting. - That is awesome.
jokes.setup: Tracy's description of the disturbing party
jokes.punchline: Frank and Toofer's contrasting reactions, with Frank doubling down
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast showing Frank's inappropriate enthusiasm vs Toofer's moral compass.

jokes.text: I left the party on a bacon run.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's at work on time
jokes.punchline: He never actually left the party, just went to get bacon
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Tracy - the casual way he mentions a 'bacon run' as if it's normal party behavior.

jokes.text: January 17th. 2007? Yeah. Ah, dammit! I knew this was going to happen.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking what date it is
jokes.punchline: Acting like the year being 2007 is a problem he anticipated
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - treating the progression of time as a personal inconvenience fits Tracy's narcissism.

jokes.text: My autobiography is due tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Tracy seems distressed and asks for paper
jokes.punchline: Treating an autobiography like a school assignment due the next day
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise - treating a major life project like homework. Perfect Tracy logic.

jokes.text: I think my snake is sick, so I need you to go out to my car and rub his belly 'til he poops.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving Jack his first assignment
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and bizarre pet care task
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate first task for corporate executive. The specificity makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: It's like having Confederate money. No one's going to take that.
jokes.setup: Complaining about getting a $100 bill from ATM
jokes.punchline: Comparing legal tender to worthless Confederate currency
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about large bills being impractical. Historical reference adds wit.

jokes.text: My friend Jenna and I didn't know your name, so we've been calling you the Hair.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to be charming
jokes.punchline: Admits to reducing him to a physical feature
jokes.explanation: Callback to opening joke, showing Liz's social awkwardness by oversharing something insulting.

jokes.text: How would you like it if I called you Glasses?
jokes.setup: Gray responding to being called 'the Hair'
jokes.punchline: Proposes equally reductive nickname
jokes.explanation: Logical comeback that sets up the next joke. Good structure but predictable.

jokes.text: I would be fine with that. Oh, good, because that's what I've been calling you.
jokes.setup: Gray's rhetorical question about calling her Glasses
jokes.punchline: Liz accepts it, then Gray reveals he already was calling her that
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that shows they're both equally shallow. Perfect misdirection structure.

jokes.text: She was just sitting there in her house coat, holding one of her boobs like this.
jokes.setup: Tracy dictating inappropriate childhood memory
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of finding his mother's nude photos
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable overshare that fits Tracy's character but relies on shock value more than wit.

jokes.text: You knew when Sonny was coming over, 'cause she would take us to the store and buy two steaks and a bottle of Nair with cocoa butter.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his mother's boyfriend visits
jokes.punchline: Specific and inappropriate preparation details
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific details that paint an uncomfortable but vivid picture. The Nair detail is perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: According to Wikipedia, you were discovered after doing stand-up at the Apollo in 1984. I have no memory of that. Write it up.
jokes.setup: Toofer fact-checking Tracy's life
jokes.punchline: Tracy doesn't remember major career moment but accepts it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - casual acceptance of not remembering his own career breakthrough. Absurdist but believable for the character.

jokes.text: Or get me a time machine so I can go back in time and smack your mom for smoking crack while she's pregnant.
jokes.setup: Jack treating Kenneth rudely while role-playing as Tracy's boss
jokes.punchline: Extremely over-the-top insult about the waiter's mother
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that shows Jack going too far in his Tracy impression. Dark but fits the show's tone.

jokes.text: Or maybe you really are the Hair and I'm the Head in our relationship.
jokes.setup: Liz worried about dating above her league
jokes.punchline: Jenna making it about herself with the Head/Hair metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna selfishness - making Liz's dating anxiety about their friendship dynamic.

jokes.text: Liz and Jenna, nothing.
jokes.setup: Jack politely offering to get coffee for the ladies
jokes.punchline: Dismissively answers his own question
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jack's transformation under Kenneth's management style.

jokes.text: That way, by the time Mr. Williams gets back from the liquor store, it's nice and tidy.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining Brian Williams' dressing room schedule
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of news anchor going to liquor store
jokes.explanation: Dark throwaway line about respected news anchor that Kenneth delivers with complete innocence.

jokes.text: Is this tube sock filled with bird seed?
jokes.setup: Jack finding strange items while cleaning
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific object that raises many questions
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific absurd detail that suggests the bizarre world of Kenneth's job.

jokes.text: Kenneth, this is not a job. This is an exercise in constant humiliation. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to help Kenneth see his job's problems
jokes.punchline: Abrupt firing as solution to workplace abuse
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jack's decisive but misguided attempt to help Kenneth.

jokes.text: So... you guys been watching Heroes? I like the Japanese dude.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to make conversation at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Awkward pop culture reference and casual description
jokes.explanation: Perfect fish-out-of-water moment showing Liz's awkwardness with sophisticated people.

jokes.text: The hors d'oeuvres tonight are boxes of pure oxygen infused with a saffron and a white truffle oil.
jokes.setup: Fancy restaurant describing appetizers
jokes.punchline: Literally serving air as expensive food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of pretentious fine dining - literally charging for flavored air.

jokes.text: I just want to go home and watch that show about midgets and eat a block of cheddar cheese.
jokes.setup: Liz feeling overwhelmed by the fancy party
jokes.punchline: Specific lowbrow comfort activities she'd prefer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - specific, relatable, and charmingly honest about her preferences.

jokes.text: And if you're trying to harvest my organs and sell them, I have an uncle who's a cop, so don't even try it.
jokes.setup: Liz listing possible ulterior motives for Gray's interest
jokes.punchline: Paranoid organ harvesting scenario with ineffective deterrent
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Liz's paranoia, and the cop uncle as protection is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Hey, we all have uncles who are cops, so just take it down a notch.
jokes.setup: Liz's organ harvesting paranoia
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal implying cop uncles are common
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflates Liz's perceived advantage while staying calm.

jokes.text: See? It says right here: 'Legal tender for all debts public and private.' Does it say anything about $100 for a bottle of water?
jokes.setup: Argument about accepting $100 bill
jokes.punchline: Clerk's sarcastic response about the specific transaction
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about the practical vs. legal aspects of currency, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: * Imagine Christmas wishes * Shooting out of your eyes * A candy cake * Full of snow dreams * A stocking full of smiles * It's a Jordan Christmas *
jokes.setup: Tracy mentions his Christmas album
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Christmas song lyrics that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly bad Christmas song that perfectly captures Tracy's ego and lack of artistic sense.

jokes.text: Although I ate way too much oxygen.
jokes.setup: Liz talking about the previous night's party
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to literally eating air
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the oxygen hors d'oeuvres, delivered with perfect Liz casualness.

jokes.text: Call in sick to work and go see a movie. A movie? A movie. With you. With me. Now. Right now.
jokes.setup: Gray suggesting something new for Liz
jokes.punchline: The idea of seeing a movie is so foreign to Liz she needs it repeated multiple times
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Liz's workaholic nature - the simple concept of leisure is foreign to her.

jokes.text: Lemon, you're looking a little under the weather. Maybe you should go home.
jokes.setup: Liz caught lying about being sick while clearly on a date
jokes.punchline: Jack sarcastically playing along with her obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's sarcastic awareness while allowing Liz her dignity.

jokes.text: Because they make television. And more than jazz, or musical theater, or morbid obesity, television is the true American art form.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why he tolerates his job
jokes.punchline: Listing morbid obesity as an American art form alongside jazz
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy disguised as patriotic speech. The casual inclusion of obesity as art form is genius.

jokes.text: From Walter Cronkite denouncing Vietnam to Oprah pulling that trash bag of fat out in a wagon.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing great TV moments
jokes.punchline: Equating historic journalism with Oprah's weight loss stunt
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of high and low culture TV moments. The specific image is both accurate and absurd.

jokes.text: From the glory and the pageantry of the Summer Olympics to the less fun Winter Olympics.
jokes.setup: Kenneth continuing his TV speech
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Winter Olympics as 'less fun'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation delivered with Kenneth's innocent honesty.

jokes.text: I want to see a show where women get their hair done, while listening to salsa music.
jokes.setup: Kenneth rejecting Jack's show ideas
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and mundane show concept
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Kenneth idea that's so specific and innocent it's funny.

jokes.text: It's called Gold Case. It's Deal or No Deal meets Millionaire.
jokes.setup: Kenneth pitching his game show idea
jokes.punchline: Generic combination of existing game shows presented as innovative
jokes.explanation: Good satire of unoriginal TV development through Kenneth's naive excitement about a derivative concept.

jokes.text: Hey, Moonvest, I got an idea for a game show last night. Give me your fingernails!
jokes.setup: Kenneth casually mentioning talking to CBS president
jokes.punchline: Demanding fingernails as bizarre business interaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from casual name-drop to completely insane demand. Perfect Kenneth energy.

jokes.text: As far as compensation goes, I would like to be officially the head of the pages, and I need a new clock radio.
jokes.setup: Kenneth negotiating his game show deal
jokes.punchline: Modest promotion paired with mundane clock radio request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - modest ambitions and practical needs in a high-stakes negotiation.

jokes.text: Also I want five points on the back end, 20% gross on merchandizing, and a creator credit on this and any international editions. And a clock radio.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's initial modest demands
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated industry terms followed by repeating the clock radio
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Kenneth knows more than he lets on, but still wants that clock radio.

jokes.text: Also I want five points on the back end, 20% gross on merchandizing, and a creator credit on this and any international editions. And a clock radio.
jokes.setup: Kenneth seeming naive about money
jokes.punchline: He suddenly becomes sophisticated about industry terms, then mentions clock radio again
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Kenneth reveals sophisticated industry knowledge while still wanting a clock radio. Perfect 30 Rock surprise.

jokes.text: Woke up in the camper at the auto show, and that's how 2006 ended.
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing his autobiography
jokes.punchline: Ending the year in a random, pathetic location
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment showing his chaotic lifestyle through specific, sad detail.

jokes.text: We do not want your book. Oops, my bad. That's on me. Shut it down.
jokes.setup: Tracy recounting publisher rejection
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of his own autobiography project
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy - treating major life project like a failed business venture he can just 'shut down.'

jokes.text: I don't really hang out with superfluously handsome gentlemen in kick-ass elevator lofts.
jokes.setup: Liz hesitating about entering Gray's apartment
jokes.punchline: Overly specific description of why this is outside her comfort zone
jokes.explanation: Good Liz line showing her self-awareness about being out of her league with specific descriptive language.

jokes.text: I don't download music without paying for it. And I never wear flip-flops. Ever. It's gross.
jokes.setup: Liz listing things she doesn't do
jokes.punchline: Equating music piracy with flip-flop wearing as moral issues
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Liz's quirky moral priorities and judgmental nature.

jokes.text: I do not sit on laps. Really? No, not a lap-sitter. Never have been.
jokes.setup: Gray encouraging Liz to try new things
jokes.punchline: Liz's weird hang-up about lap-sitting delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific Liz neurosis that's both relatable and absurd. Great character writing.

jokes.text: Why do you have a picture of my great-aunt Dolly?
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Liz and Gray
jokes.punchline: Discovery that ruins everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect sitcom moment - just when things are going well, everything falls apart in the most awkward way.

jokes.text: Franklin and Eleanor Roosevelt were fifth cousins. Okay, on the count of three, say what level of cousins we would have to be for this to be okay. One, two, three. Fifth. Unacceptable, no matter what.
jokes.setup: Gray trying to rationalize their family connection
jokes.punchline: Liz's absolute rejection despite the historical precedent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing different comfort levels with family relationships and Liz's moral rigidity.

jokes.text: You are a Hair, Liz Lemon. It's in our blood. Accept it. Embrace it.
jokes.setup: Gray trying to encourage Liz after relationship failure
jokes.punchline: Using their family connection to explain her attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good callback that provides character growth moment for Liz while maintaining the episode's central metaphor.

jokes.text: Congratulations. You struck gold on Gold Case. Oh, gold's real heavy, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Game show where contestant wins gold
jokes.punchline: Revealing the practical problems with the prize
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy revealing the flaw in Kenneth's game show concept.

jokes.text: This is pathetic. A joke. This whole show is a joke.
jokes.setup: Jack frustrated with Kenneth's failed game show
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing the actual TV show we're watching
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment where Jack's criticism applies to both the game show and 30 Rock itself.
